steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says summer squash "doesn't closely resemble any wild plant," and then states that genetic research "only recently ______ its ancestor to be the wild johnny gourd." The phrase "only recently" signals a new discovery. The blank must mean something like uncovered or made known for the first time.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'revealed': "Revealed" means disclosed something previously unknown. Genetic research recently revealed the ancestor's identity — exactly what the passage describes. ✓
  • Choice B — 'expanded': "Expanded" means made larger or broader. Research cannot expand an ancestor, and the sentence is about identifying a relationship, not enlarging one. ✗
  • Choice C — 'encouraged': "Encouraged" means promoted or gave support to. Research does not encourage an ancestor to exist; the blank needs a word for a discovery, not an act of support. ✗
  • Choice D — 'petitioned': "Petitioned" means formally requested something from an authority. Genetic research makes scientific findings; it does not file requests. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
